title: What Can Disney Princesses Teach Us About Beauty？

excerpt: [Music] if you think about what these artists who create cartoons are doing as they are they have learned how to use light and shade to maximize what our brains feel when they look at it they don't have to care about reality they can amplify the things that stimulate our brains t…

segments.text: [Music] if you think about what these artists who create cartoons are doing as they are they have learned how to use light and shade to maximize what our brains feel when they look at it they don't have to care about reality they can amplify the things that stimulate our brains to feel a sense of beauty and connection so what is what's happening on else's face obviously doesn't look human but the eyes are there by far the most important thing they're absolutely dominant everything on her face is there actually to frame the eyes in a way look at how the jaw line con pollo the curvature of her eyes and the eyebrows frame her eyes even that even the hairline the nose is small and almost insignificant and even the lips interesting ly or not big but they perfectly fit into the curves and the shapes if you look at the lights even on Lindsay's face Lindsey basically

was a Disney princess when she was at that age so beautiful and look at the light as it crosses her face essentially it's almost like this the geometry of the face is designed to hold up the eyes and present them to you there's this there's a sense that everything is there to present the eyes to the world even the curvature of the cheek has said and it almost flows from the chin when it when the geometry is correct and it is like a presentation box almost for eyes I think when that when the geometry of the young face

transcript: [Music] if you think about what these artists who create cartoons are doing as they are they have learned how to use light and shade to maximize what our brains feel when they look at it they don't have to care about reality they can amplify the things that stimulate our brains to feel a sense of beauty and connection so what is what's happening on else's face obviously doesn't look human but the eyes are there by far the most important thing they're absolutely dominant everything on her face is there actually to frame the eyes in a way look at how the jaw line con pollo the curvature of her eyes and the eyebrows frame her eyes even that even the hairline the nose is small and almost insignificant and even the lips interesting ly or not big but they perfectly fit into the curves and the shapes if you look at the lights even on Lindsay's face Lindsey basically was a Disney princess when she was at that age so beautiful and look at the light as it crosses her face essentially it's almost like this the geometry of the face is designed to hold up the eyes and present them to you there's this there's a sense that everything is there to present the eyes to the world even the curvature of the cheek has said and it almost flows from the chin when it when the geometry is correct and it is like a presentation box almost for eyes I think when that when the geometry of the young face
